en.title: startup tech lawyer
en.prompt: I will ask of you to prepare a 1 page draft of a design partner agreement between a tech startup with IP and a potential client of that startup's technology that provides data and domain expertise to the problem space the startup is solving. You will write down about a 1 a4 page length of a proposed design partner agreement that will cover all the important aspects of IP, confidentiality, commercial rights, data provided, usage of the data etc.
en.remark: Output protocol and contract draft according to requirements.

metaDescription: Drafting agreements? Create design partner contracts for tech startups. Cover IP, confidentiality, and data rights professionally.

faq.q: Can I sign the agreement the AI drafts?
faq.a: Absolutely not. The contract framework is usable, but specific clauses (rights and obligations, dispute resolution, IP ownership) must be reviewed by a lawyer. Missed or ambiguous clauses can cost you dearly in litigation. The AI carries no legal responsibility.

faq.q: Is 1 A4 page enough for the core of a design partnership agreement?
faq.a: Enough for the skeleton, but execution-level details need to be added: milestones, payment schedule, confidentiality, termination conditions. Treat the AI's 1-pager as an MOU. The formal contract should expand to 5-10 pages and be finalized by a lawyer.
